title: Modal dialog vs confirmation dialog
summary: A confirmation dialog is a specific modal decision pattern. Not every modal is a confirmation, and not every interruption deserves modal treatment.
decisionRules: Use a modal dialog for a focused task that must happen in a separate temporary mode.
Use a confirmation dialog only when the decision protects against a meaningful consequence.
Use inline or nonmodal feedback when the message does not require an immediate decision.
failureModes: Treating modal as a generic container for any extra content.
Using vague confirmation copy that fails to name the consequence.

sources.note: The APG dialog and alert dialog patterns separate modal behavior from alerting semantics.
Apple guidance frames alerts as interruptive and appropriate only for critical information.
